functional_purpose: Accepts an HTTP request containing an OpenEyes patient id, resolves the hospital number from the OpenEyes database, retrieves AIS flags from OpenEyes, turns them into HL7 IAM segments, and routes each result to the AIS Sender channel.
business_process: Makes patient AIS flags available to the downstream AIS workflow.
source_system: HTTP client requesting AIS flags for an OpenEyes patient

destination_connectors.name: Get Flags
destination_connectors.endpoint: OpenEyes PASAPI path supplied by request

protocol: HTTP / HTTPS and internal channel routing
ack_behaviour: HTTP listener uses auto-generated response before processing; no MLLP ACK is configured.
main_transformer_stages: Extract request URL parameter and patient id
Query OpenEyes database for hospital number
Build HL7 ADT header and PID
Convert returned AISFlag values to IAM segments
Route each IAM segment to AIS Sender
error_handling: Logs database-query errors; removes all destinations when no hospital number is found.
queue_behaviour: HTTP source buffer 1000; both destinations have queueing disabled.
logging: Logs database errors and missing hospital number; no explicit patient value is logged.

evidence.claim: MEH AIS OUT (8439efef-2f9a-46c9-9385-888b4c2d0b92) receives HTTP on port 6664 and extracts a patient id from request parameters.
evidence.location: MEH/AIS OUT.xml, source connector and URL/patientId transformer lines 10-18, 52-82

evidence.claim: MEH AIS OUT (8439efef-2f9a-46c9-9385-888b4c2d0b92) queries the OpenEyes patient identifier tables and suppresses destinations when the hospital number is absent.
evidence.location: MEH/AIS OUT.xml, hospNum JavaScript transformer lines 84-123

evidence.claim: MEH AIS OUT (8439efef-2f9a-46c9-9385-888b4c2d0b92) GETs the requested OpenEyes path with preemptive HTTP Basic authentication, transforms AISFlag values into IAM segments, then routes them to AIS Sender.
evidence.location: MEH/AIS OUT.xml, Get Flags HTTP Sender lines 193-243 and response transformer lines 303-328; Send A60 writer lines 370-401

functional_purpose: Receives internally routed HL7 messages and sends only messages containing IAM segments to a remote MLLP receiver.
business_process: Delivers AIS flag update segments to the receiving clinical system.
ack_behaviour: TCP sender waits for a response and uses MLLP ACK/NACK bytes; response timeout is 5 seconds.
main_filters: Send only if the message contains at least one IAM segment
retry_behaviour: Destination queue disabled and retryCount 0; MLLP maxRetries is 2.

evidence.claim: MEH AIS Sender (8c65e82d-cad1-44e9-b56e-efd400d726af) has a Channel Reader source, establishing an internal-channel input rather than an external listener.
evidence.location: MEH/AIS Sender.xml, source connector lines 10-14 and 113-115

evidence.claim: MEH AIS Sender (8c65e82d-cad1-44e9-b56e-efd400d726af) filters for IAM segments and sends the encoded HL7 message through an MLLP TCP sender.
evidence.location: MEH/AIS Sender.xml, Destination 1 TCP properties lines 144-169 and filter/transport lines 338-352

evidence.claim: MEH AIS Sender (8c65e82d-cad1-44e9-b56e-efd400d726af) has disabled destination queueing, retryCount 0, and MLLP maxRetries 2.
evidence.location: MEH/AIS Sender.xml, Destination 1 properties lines 124-152

functional_purpose: Receives DICOM instances, derives patient, device, and AE-title metadata, submits every instance to the OpenEyes PayloadProcessor DICOM queue, and additionally writes supported Zeiss IOLMaster instances to the IOLMasterImport folder.
business_process: Ingests ophthalmic imaging and routes IOLMaster biometry for import.

destination_connectors.name: Write out file to IolMasterImport incoming folder when device is a Zeiss IOLMaster

ack_behaviour: DICOM listener auto-generates its response after the source transformer.
main_filters: Remove IOLMaster file destination unless manufacturer contains Zeiss, model contains IOLMaster, and SOP class is supported
main_transformer_stages: Extract patient, study, device, and AE-title DICOM tags
Derive identifier type and institution id from AE title or configurationMap default
Set source platform from AE title
Suppress IOLMaster file route when device/SOP criteria do not match
retry_behaviour: Both destinations have queueing disabled and retryCount 0.
queue_behaviour: DICOM source buffer 1000; destination queueing disabled.
logging: No custom logging; message and DICOM attachment content are retained for 14 days, creating PII risk.

evidence.claim: MEH DICOM (319fcf7f-ef08-4564-9dd1-33be634edfd4) listens for DICOM on port 11112 and maps patient, study, device, and AE-title values from DICOM tags.
evidence.location: MEH/DICOM.xml, DICOM Listener lines 10-18 and source transformer lines 63-202

evidence.claim: MEH DICOM (319fcf7f-ef08-4564-9dd1-33be634edfd4) reads configurationMap defaultPatientIdentifierType and removes the IOLMaster file destination unless the device and SOP class meet the Zeiss IOLMaster criteria.
evidence.location: MEH/DICOM.xml, source transformer scripts lines 203-267

evidence.claim: MEH DICOM (319fcf7f-ef08-4564-9dd1-33be634edfd4) writes selected files to /mnt/dicom and POSTs application/dicom with request_type dicom_request to OpenEyes /api/v1/request/queue/add using preemptive HTTP Basic authentication.
evidence.location: MEH/DICOM.xml, File Writer lines 287-351 and PayloadProcessor API Send lines 353-470

evidence.claim: MEH DICOM (319fcf7f-ef08-4564-9dd1-33be634edfd4) stores DICOM attachments and retains message content for 14 days.
evidence.location: MEH/DICOM.xml, channel properties and export metadata lines 487-533

functional_purpose: Polls a local document folder and transfers each binary file unchanged to a remote SFTP document location using its original filename.
business_process: Exports document-management files to a remote document share.
main_transformer_stages: Pass through raw binary data and original filename
error_handling: Moves source files to /mnt/docman/error when an error response occurs; source files are deleted after processing.
queue_behaviour: Source polls every 5 seconds; destination queueing disabled.
logging: No custom logging; raw document content is stored, creating PII risk.

evidence.claim: MEH Docman (04524f4d-5201-4c16-b66f-26c3d7783d85) is a File Reader polling /mnt/docman every 5 seconds for binary files, deleting successfully processed files and moving error responses to an error folder.
evidence.location: MEH/Docman.xml, source File Reader properties lines 10-80 and transport lines 99-105

evidence.claim: MEH Docman (04524f4d-5201-4c16-b66f-26c3d7783d85) writes raw encoded data with the original filename to an SFTP destination using key authentication.
evidence.location: MEH/Docman.xml, Destination 1 File Writer properties lines 110-160 and transport lines 197-203

evidence.claim: MEH Docman (04524f4d-5201-4c16-b66f-26c3d7783d85) has destination queueing disabled, retryCount 0, and stores attachments/message content.
evidence.location: MEH/Docman.xml, destination properties lines 113-131 and channel properties lines 217-243

functional_purpose: Accepts HTTP patient-query or XML request data, builds an HL7 query/event message, sends it by MLLP to a remote PAS, converts its response to XML, and returns the transformed response to the HTTP caller.
business_process: Supports patient lookup and patient-flow query/response between OpenEyes-facing callers and the remote PAS.
message_format: HTTP XML to HL7v2 to XML
ack_behaviour: HTTP source responds after processing using response variable d6; MLLP sender waits up to 60 seconds for a response.
main_filters: Pass messages with populated location or QPD patient query identifier, or A03
main_transformer_stages: Set MSH time and control id
Set QPD identifier from hosnum or nhsnum
Convert optional XML request into HL7 segments and map location status codes
Read Send Q21 response as HL7 XML
Apply XSLT to construct response XML
error_handling: If no PAS response is present, builds a fallback QBP/Q21-shaped message before transforming; invalid location codes are logged.
retry_behaviour: All destinations have queueing disabled and retryCount 0; MLLP maxRetries is 2.
queue_behaviour: HTTP source buffer 1000; destination queueing disabled.
logging: Logs request JSON, PAS response message, and response length; these can contain PII.

evidence.claim: MEH OpenEyes PAS Query v2 (969561b8-b516-4fce-9b3e-6865d6f19602) is an HTTP Listener on port 6663 that responds after processing via response variable d6 and constructs HL7 from request parameters/XML.
evidence.location: MEH/OpenEyes PAS Query v2.xml, source connector lines 10-49 and source transformer lines 52-200

evidence.claim: MEH OpenEyes PAS Query v2 (969561b8-b516-4fce-9b3e-6865d6f19602) sends filtered HL7 by MLLP to a remote TCP endpoint, accepting query identifiers/location data or A03 events.
evidence.location: MEH/OpenEyes PAS Query v2.xml, Send Q21 TCP properties lines 265-313 and filter lines 485-537

evidence.claim: MEH OpenEyes PAS Query v2 (969561b8-b516-4fce-9b3e-6865d6f19602) reads the Send Q21 response, converts it to XML, applies an enabled XSLT response transform, and returns the transformed response or an empty PatientList.
evidence.location: MEH/OpenEyes PAS Query v2.xml, Convert K21 to XML writer lines 541-583; Create response/XSLT lines 693-725; Respond writer lines 983-1009

functional_purpose: Receives HL7 ADT messages by MLLP and updates OpenEyes patient records, appointments, patient merges, and AIS flags through PASAPI endpoints selected by message event and patient content.
business_process: Synchronizes PAS demographic, encounter, appointment, merge, and AIS flag changes into OpenEyes.
ack_behaviour: TCP source is MLLP and auto-generates an ACK before processing; it is configured with ACK AA, error AE, and rejection AR codes.
main_filters: Secondary patient and patient destinations filter A40
Appointment PUT filters A01/A02/A03/A04/A05/A08/A11/A12/A13 except A08 at CANCL
main_transformer_stages: Transform HL7 patient data to PASAPI XML
Map patient and visit identifiers, dates, clinic, doctor, visit status, NHS number, and AIS XML
Extract patient id and warning information from PASAPI responses
error_handling: Transformer mapping exceptions are logged; destination response transformers inspect warnings and patient id.
retry_behaviour: All HTTP destinations have queueing disabled and retryCount 0.
queue_behaviour: MLLP source buffer 1000; destination queueing disabled.
logging: Logs transformer mapping errors and one PD1 field value, creating PII risk.

evidence.claim: MEH OpenEyes PAS v2 (7d6b6b87-29ff-49e2-b4e5-38022c768b24) listens for HL7v2 over MLLP on port 6661 and is configured to auto-generate AA/AE/AR acknowledgements before processing.
evidence.location: MEH/OpenEyes PAS v2.xml, source TCP Listener and MLLP/HL7 properties lines 10-140

evidence.claim: MEH OpenEyes PAS v2 (7d6b6b87-29ff-49e2-b4e5-38022c768b24) POSTs secondary patient data and PUTs patient data to PASAPI V2 Patient endpoints with preemptive HTTP Basic authentication, using transformer-derived XML.
evidence.location: MEH/OpenEyes PAS v2.xml, PASAPI - Secondary Patient lines 148-616 and PASAPI - Patient lines 623-1169

evidence.claim: MEH OpenEyes PAS v2 (7d6b6b87-29ff-49e2-b4e5-38022c768b24) PUTs/deletes PASAPI V2 PatientAppointment records for configured ADT events and PUTs a PASAPI V1 PatientMerge only for A38.
evidence.location: MEH/OpenEyes PAS v2.xml, appointment filters/endpoints lines 1176-1702 and Patient Merge endpoint/filter lines 1705-1948

evidence.claim: MEH OpenEyes PAS v2 (7d6b6b87-29ff-49e2-b4e5-38022c768b24) PUTs AIS XML to PASAPI V2 AISFlags when PID.35.1 exists.
evidence.location: MEH/OpenEyes PAS v2.xml, AIS HTTP Sender and filter lines 1950-2116
